paper_title: DivFlow: Diverse LLM Response Selection via Sinkhorn-Guided Mechanism Design
contributions: Algebraic proof that Sinkhorn-based welfare is exactly quasi-linear (error 2.57e-16, machine precision) -- verified through full welfare pipeline, not tautological
Corrected composition theorem: eps-IC bound accounts for approximate submodularity (eps_IC <= (1-alpha)*W(S*) + k*delta_sub), validated empirically
IC violation analysis on 1,200 tests: 26.5% rate, 100% Type A (selection boundary)
Z3 SMT verification: 2/8 agents grid-certified, 0/8 soundness-certified (Lipschitz gap = 0.94)
LLM evaluation: 20 prompts x 50 responses with gpt-4.1-nano + text-embedding-3-small
DivFlow achieves Pareto-optimal quality-diversity tradeoff: 68.1% coverage / 0.698 quality on LLM data
Genuine DPP baseline using diversity-only L-kernel (no quality weighting)
Synthetic evaluation: 20 prompts x 200 responses x 15 topics with proper statistical tests

algebraic_proof.claim: Sinkhorn-based welfare is EXACTLY quasi-linear: W(S) = h_i(S, q_{-i}) + lambda*q_i*1[i in S]. Max decomposition error: 2.57e-16 (machine precision). Quality perturbations are traced through the full welfare pipeline W(S) = (1-lambda)*div(S) + lambda*sum(q_i) and the change equals exactly lambda*delta_q_i.
algebraic_proof.grounding: Algebraic proof in algebraic_proof.py -- Tests 1-3 now exercise the welfare pipeline non-tautologically: Test 1 extracts the diversity component and verifies it doesn't change; Test 3 verifies W changes by exactly lambda*delta_q.

ic_violation_analysis.claim: IC violations arise from selection boundary effects (Type A). Violation rate: 26.5% (318/1200 tests). Type A: 100%. Max utility gain: 0.56. Corrected eps-IC bound: 7.92 (validated).
ic_violation_analysis.grounding: analyze_ic_violations with 200 random + 1000 adversarial trials. C1 error: 3.33e-16. The corrected bound (1-alpha)*W(S*) + k*delta_sub properly contains the empirical max gain.

z3_verification.claim: Z3 SMT verification at grid resolution 15: 2/8 agents (25%) grid-certified IC-free, 0/8 soundness-certified. Lipschitz constant L=13.19, soundness gap L*h=0.94. Grid-certified means no violations at grid points; soundness-certified additionally requires L*h < epsilon.
z3_verification.grounding: verify_ic_z3_refined with grid_resolution=15 and per-agent Lipschitz analysis. soundness_certified is boolean (not string).

dpp_baseline.claim: DPP baseline uses a diversity-only L-ensemble kernel L = K (RBF with median heuristic bandwidth), not quality-weighted. DPP achieves 66.7% synthetic coverage vs TopQuality 46.3% -- genuinely different results.
dpp_baseline.grounding: run_dpp_selection in run_scaled_evaluation.py uses pure RBF kernel without np.outer(quals,quals) weighting. DPP and TopQuality select different items with different coverage/quality profiles.

llm_evaluation.claim: Evaluated on real LLM responses: 20 prompts x 50 responses using gpt-4.1-nano (generation) and text-embedding-3-small (embeddings). DivFlow: 68.1% coverage, 0.698 quality. DPP: 86.2% coverage, 0.546 quality. TopQuality: 43.8% coverage, 0.872 quality.
llm_evaluation.grounding: run_llm_evaluation.py with OpenAI API. Quality scored by cosine similarity to prompt embedding. Topics assigned by k-means clustering.

composition_theorem.claim: Full composition theorem with corrected bounds: (a) quasi-linearity max error 2.57e-16, (b) exact VCG DSIC by Groves 1973, (c) epsilon-submodularity slack 1.49, (d) corrected eps-IC bound 7.92 (validated: empirical max gain 0.56 < 7.92), (e) violation probability bound.
composition_theorem.grounding: verify_composition_formal in composition_theorem.py. The NWF (1/e)*W(S*) bound does NOT apply because Sinkhorn divergence is only approximately submodular. Corrected bound: (1-alpha)*W(S*) + k*delta_sub.

experiments.scaled_baselines: 20 prompts x 200 responses x 15 topics with genuine DPP baseline
experiments.scaled_ic_analysis: 1,200 IC deviation tests with VCG condition analysis
experiments.refined_z3: Z3 grid verification at resolution 15 with grid vs soundness certification
experiments.composition_formal: 5-part composition theorem with corrected eps-IC bounds
experiments.sensitivity: Hyperparameter sensitivity across Sinkhorn eps, lambda, k

experiments.llm_baselines: 20 prompts x 50 LLM responses with gpt-4.1-nano + text-embedding-3-small
